req.title: Hr-Admin updates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a timesheet through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:hr-admin
timesheet module is enabled

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated hr-admin can update a timesheet via REST API with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. I apply EP to cover the valid hr-admin update path, NEG for unauthenticated and forbidden-role paths, BVA for boundary conditions on a numeric field (e.g., hours worked at min/max limits), STC to verify the state transition from draft to submitted timesheet, and DT to cover the decision matrix of auth × role × payload validity. Each test is self-contained using fixtures and helper placeholders so no shared mutable state exists between runs.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_valid_payload_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with known id exists in a updatable state
actions: Obtain a valid JWT for hr-admin role via <get_auth_token> fixture
Retrieve an existing timesheet id via <create_or_fetch_timesheet> fixture
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 response
Assert response body contains entity id
Assert audit record exists via <verify_audit_record> helper
expected: Response JSON contains 'id' matching the updated timesheet
Audit record for this timesheet update exists in the audit log
trace: R-WEB-018: audit record written on success
R-WEB-018: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_update_timesheet_valid_payload_returns_200_and_audit(
    hr_admin_token,
    existing_timesheet_id,
    api_base_url,
    verify_audit_record,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP-001",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-07",
        "hours_worked": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}"
    response = requests.put(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response body missing 'id' field"
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(existing_timesheet_id)
    assert verify_audit_record(entity_id=existing_timesheet_id, action="update"), "Audit record not found"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_update_timesheet_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is provided
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with known id exists
actions: Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} without Authorization header
Assert HTTP 401 response
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_update_timesheet_returns_401(
    existing_timesheet_id,
    api_base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP-001",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-07",
        "hours_worked": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}"
    response = requests.put(url, json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert any(k in body for k in ("error", "message", "detail")), "No error key in 401 body"

a timesheet with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid JWT for employee role via <get_auth_token> fixture with role='employee'
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with a valid payload
Assert HTTP 403 response

):

    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP-001",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-07",
        "hours_worked": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}"
    response = requests.put(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert any(k in body for k in ("error", "message", "detail")), "No error key in 403 body"

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid JWT for hr-admin role via <get_auth_token> fixture
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with mandatory field 'employee_id' omitted
Assert HTTP 400 response
Assert response body contains validation error detail
expected: Response body identifies 'employee_id' as a missing or invalid field
trace: R-WEB-018: error pathway 400 on validation error
R-WEB-018: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    hr_admin_token,
    existing_timesheet_id,
    api_base_url,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-07",
        "hours_worked": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/timesheets/{existing_timesheet_id}"
    response = requests.put(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    body_str = str(body).lower()
    assert "employee_id" in body_str or "validation" in body_str, "Missing field error not in body"

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_hours_at_minimum_boundary_returns_success
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid JWT for hr-admin role via <get_auth_token> fixture
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with hours_worked set to minimum valid value (0)
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 response
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with hours_worked set to -1 (below minimum)
Assert HTTP 400 response
trace: R-WEB-018: mandatory field validation at numeric boundary
R-WEB-018: error pathway 400 on validation error

tests.name: test_update_timesheet_draft_to_submitted_state_transition
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
timesheet module is enabled
a timesheet in 'draft' state exists with known id
actions: Obtain a valid JWT for hr-admin role via <get_auth_token> fixture
Confirm current timesheet status is 'draft' via GET /api/timesheets/{id}
Send PUT /api/timesheets/{id} with status='submitted' and all mandatory fields
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 response
Assert response or follow-up GET shows status='submitted'
Assert audit record reflects state change
expected: Audit record captures the state transition
trace: R-WEB-018: audit record written on success
R-WEB-018: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_timesheet_draft_to_submitted_state_transition(
    hr_admin_token,
    draft_timesheet_id,
    api_base_url,
    verify_audit_record,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/timesheets/{draft_timesheet_id}"

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "draft", "Precondition: timesheet must be in draft state"

    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP-001",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-07",
        "hours_worked": 40,
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {put_resp.status_code}"

    verify_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert verify_resp.json().get("status") == "submitted", "Status did not transition to submitted"
    assert verify_audit_record(entity_id=draft_timesheet_id, action="update"), "Audit record not found"
